survey-engine-l2.extractKeyPhrase: _extractKeyPhrase() — extract 2-5 key words from user answer
survey-engine-l2.processAnswerL2: L1 shallow → L2 formatted question, L2 deep → next subsection, L2 shallow → L3
survey-engine-l2.reflectionToL2: Shallow correction during reflection → L2 with formatted question

tests: tests/test-depth-levels.js (renamed from test-l1-depth.js, 36 tests all pass)

survey-engine-l3.formatL3Question: _formatL3Question(subsectionKey, fullContext) — root cause question with full dialogue context reference (L1+L2)
survey-engine-l3.processAnswerL3: L2 shallow → L3 with formatted question + fullContext, L3 deep → depthReached L3 → next, L3 shallow → depthReached L3 max → next (no loop)
survey-engine-l3.l3ContextRelevance: L3 question references full dialogue via _extractKeyPhrase() — "вы говорили про [ключевая фраза]"
survey-engine-l3.l3NoLoop: L3 is max depth — even shallow answer leads to transition, no infinite loop

artifactsCreated.prompt-l3: agents/questioner/prompt.md — L3 section enhanced: no irritation tone, no loop rule, full context relevance
artifactsCreated.tests-l3: tests/test-depth-levels.js (56 tests all pass — 36 original + 20 new L3 tests)

survey-engine-return.returnToSection: Enhanced returnToSection(blockId, subId, options) — saves return origin, shows "ранее вы ответили" with previous answer, supports isUserBack/reason for /back vs controller-initiated return
survey-engine-return.handleBackNavigation: handleBackNavigation(session) — uses TemplateEngine to find previous subsection, calls returnToSection, returns atStart:true when at first subsection
survey-engine-return.continueFromReturn: continueFromReturn(session, subId) — restores origin position after user refines a returned-to section, preserves Session Context state
survey-engine-return._handleReturnAnswer: _handleReturnAnswer(session, answer, subId) — saves refinement answer, preserves previous depth, collects risks, calls continueFromReturn
survey-engine-return.getAvailableReturnTargets: getAvailableReturnTargets(session) — returns list of all answered subsections for controller-initiated navigation
survey-engine-return._getPreviousSubsection: _getPreviousSubsection(session) — finds previous subsection in traversal order for /back command
survey-engine-return._formatReturnQuestion: _formatReturnQuestion() — uses "ранее вы ответили:" phrase, supports isUserBack and reason options
survey-engine-return.processAnswerReturnDetection: processAnswer() detects _returnActive flag and routes to _handleReturnAnswer() for return-flow answers

session-commands-return.surveyEngineIntegration: constructor accepts surveyEngine dep; handleBackConfirm uses surveyEngine.handleBackNavigation() with fallback to legacy logic
session-commands-return.Ранее вы ответили: handleBackCommand uses "ранее вы ответили" phrasing in both confirmation and navigation messages
session-commands-return.fallbackLegacyLogic: Preserved legacy back navigation logic as fallback when surveyEngine is unavailable

features: Title page: project name, date, author
Table of contents with depth indicators
7 blocks with professional styling (Calibri, margins, colors)
Risk section: summary table + detailed by category
Retry logic (up to 3 attempts with exponential backoff)

artifactsCreated.prompt-generator: agents/generator/prompt.md — generator agent system prompt with input/output spec, DOCX structure, formatting rules, anti-patterns

link-sharing.signalCompletion: signalCompletion() enhanced — accepts links (docxUrl, htmlUrl), builds final message with DOCX + web links, fallback on publication failure

github-pages.publishDraft: publishDraft(sessionId, docxPath, htmlPath, projectName) — full publish flow: validate files → ensure worktree → copy files → commit & push → return URLs
github-pages._commitAndPush: git add -A, commit 'Add BRD session {sessionId} - {projectName}', push to gh-pages (with retry)
github-pages._ensureWorktree: Clones pages repo if not exists, else pull (graceful if no remote)
github-pages._hasRemote: git remote get-url check — skips fetch if no remote
github-pages.dryRun: Config option — skips git push for testing
github-pages.config: Env: GITHUB_PAGES_REPO, GITHUB_TOKEN, GITHUB_PAGES_DIR, GITHUB_PAGES_BRANCH, GITHUB_PAGES_URL
github-pages.tests: tests/test-github-pages.js — 33 tests covering config, validation, file copy, URL generation, commit messages, integration, edge cases

generator-index.publishFlow: agents/generator/index.js — GeneratorAgent.process() now calls publisher.publishDraft() after DOCX+HTML generation. Falls back gracefully if GitHub Pages not configured. New publishFromResult() method.

Sample data integrity: blocks, subsections, depth labels, risks
HTML generation: all 7 blocks, depth badges, risk section, TOC
DOCX generation: title page, TOC, blocks, risk section
Content identity: same key phrases, numbers, structure in both formats
Subsection labels: same subsectionId/subsectionName in both

Risk data: same risk counts, categories, mitigations in both
